data.description:
## Reproduction Steps
1. Close a work ticket with verify.require_completion_verification_for_close=true.
2. For some inputs (deterministic on b593, a tiny pyproject diff) the verify-step model emits a top-level JSON **array** (bare findings list) instead of the completion_verdict **object**.
3. src/rebar/llm/structured.py validate_to() raises 'structured output must be a JSON object, got list'; step 'decide@then/verify' fails and the close is blocked fail-closed.

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected: verifier tolerates/normalizes a single verdict wrapped in a list (or forces object output), producing PASS/FAIL.
- Actual: deterministic parse failure (5/5); ticket cannot earn a signed attestation, must be --force-closed.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tolerant_parse/validate_to robustly unwraps a top-level array carrying one verdict object, OR the verify step constrains output to an object.
- [ ] Regression test reproduces the 'got list' input and asserts a verdict.
- [ ] A previously-affected ticket earns a signed completion-verifier PASS.
data.title: completion-verifier: top-level JSON array from verify model fails to parse (validate_to 'got list')
